instructions: Read the question. Then arrange the scrambled words to complete the sentence that answers it. One word may be extra — do not use it.

questions.prompt: What did she ask about your future plans?
questions.fullSentence: She wanted to know which colleges I'm considering.
questions.explanation: 'Considering' is the extra word. The correct structure is: She wanted to know which colleges I'm considering.

questions.prompt: What did the teacher tell you about the exam?
questions.fullSentence: She said the exam would be held next week.
questions.explanation: 'Postponed' is the extra word. The sentence needs 'held' not 'postponed' to say the exam would be held next week.

questions.prompt: What did your friend explain about being late?
questions.fullSentence: He explained that he had missed the bus.
questions.explanation: 'Unfortunately' is the extra word. It is an adverb that doesn't fit in the blank position.

questions.prompt: What did the manager announce at the meeting?
questions.fullSentence: She announced that the office would close on Friday.
questions.explanation: 'Shut' is the extra word. 'Close' is the correct verb here.

questions.prompt: What did your roommate ask you to do before leaving?
questions.fullSentence: She reminded me to turn off the lights.
questions.explanation: 'Switch' is the extra word. 'Turn off' is the standard phrase used with lights.

questions.prompt: What did your doctor advise you?
questions.fullSentence: My doctor advised me to drink more water.
questions.explanation: 'Consuming' and 'that' are not needed. The structure is 'advise someone to do something'.

questions.prompt: What did the professor warn the students about?
questions.fullSentence: He warned us not to miss the deadlines.
questions.explanation: 'Ignore' is the extra word. 'Miss the deadlines' is the correct phrase.

questions.prompt: What did your classmate ask about the assignment?
questions.fullSentence: She asked whether we could work in pairs.
questions.explanation: 'Submit' and 'together' are extra. 'Work in pairs' is the intended phrase.

questions.prompt: What did the announcement say about the library?
questions.fullSentence: It said the library would be closed Saturday.
questions.explanation: 'Shut' is the extra word. 'Closed' is the appropriate adjective here.

questions.prompt: What did your neighbor mention about the noise?
questions.fullSentence: He mentioned that the loud music bothered him at night.
questions.explanation: 'Disturbed' is the extra word. 'Bothered' fits naturally in the sentence.

questions.prompt: What did your supervisor say about the report?
questions.fullSentence: She told me to submit the report by Friday.
questions.explanation: 'Finish' is the extra word. The sentence uses 'submit' as the main verb.

questions.prompt: What did the email from HR say?
questions.fullSentence: It stated that the training attendance was mandatory.
questions.explanation: 'Required' is the extra word — 'mandatory' already expresses the same idea.

questions.prompt: What did your friend say about the restaurant?
questions.fullSentence: She said she had never been there before.
questions.explanation: 'Visited' is extra. 'Been there' is the natural idiom for having gone somewhere.

questions.prompt: What did your cousin tell you about the job?
questions.fullSentence: He told me he was applying for the position.
questions.explanation: 'Interested' is the extra word. The sentence is about applying, not interest.

questions.prompt: What did the sign at the entrance say?
questions.fullSentence: It said that smoking was strictly prohibited.
questions.explanation: 'Entirely' is the extra word. 'Strictly prohibited' is the standard phrase.

questions.prompt: What did your friend suggest for the weekend?
questions.fullSentence: She suggested that we go hiking together.
questions.explanation: 'Travel' is the extra word. The suggestion is specifically about hiking.

questions.prompt: What did the coach tell the team after the game?
questions.fullSentence: He told us we needed to train harder.
questions.explanation: 'Practice' is the extra word. 'Train harder' is the complete phrase here.

questions.prompt: What did the receptionist say about the appointment?
questions.fullSentence: She said the doctor would be available at 3 PM.
questions.explanation: 'Free' is the extra word. 'Available' is the more appropriate professional term here.

questions.prompt: What did your professor remind you about?
questions.fullSentence: She reminded us to proofread our essays before submitting.
questions.explanation: 'Review' is the extra word. 'Proofread' is the specific action the professor recommended.

questions.prompt: What did the notice on the door say?
questions.fullSentence: It said the office would remain closed until Monday.
questions.explanation: 'Stay' is the extra word. 'Remain closed' is the standard formal expression.

questions.prompt: What did your landlord mention about the repairs?
questions.fullSentence: He mentioned that they would be fixed the following week.
questions.explanation: 'Completed' is the extra word. 'Be fixed' is the correct passive construction.

questions.prompt: What did your classmate ask about the exam schedule?
questions.fullSentence: She asked if the exam had really been postponed.
questions.explanation: 'Rescheduled' is the extra word. The sentence already ends with 'postponed'.

questions.prompt: What did the customer service rep explain?
questions.fullSentence: She explained that the refund would be processed within 5 days.
questions.explanation: 'Returned' is the extra word. 'Processed' is the correct term for a refund.

questions.prompt: What did the guide tell tourists about the museum?
questions.fullSentence: He told us the museum was closed on Mondays.
questions.explanation: 'Shut' is the extra word. 'Closed' is the standard adjective used for venues.

questions.prompt: What did your sister say about her new job?
questions.fullSentence: She said she was really excited about her new role.
questions.explanation: 'Enjoying' is the extra word. 'Excited about' is the correct collocation.

questions.prompt: What did the flight attendant announce before landing?
questions.fullSentence: She announced that passengers should return to their seats.
questions.explanation: 'Buckle' is the extra word. The announcement is about returning to seats.

questions.prompt: What did your parents ask you about your grades?
questions.fullSentence: They asked me whether I was actually studying enough.
questions.explanation: 'Really' is the extra word. 'Actually' is sufficient as the adverb here.

questions.prompt: What did the email from the registrar say?
questions.fullSentence: It said all forms must be submitted by noon.
questions.explanation: 'Filed' is the extra word. 'Submitted' is the correct term in this academic context.

questions.prompt: What did your coworker mention about the meeting?
questions.fullSentence: She mentioned that it was not optional to attend.
questions.explanation: 'Mandatory' is the extra word. 'Not optional' already conveys the same meaning.

questions.prompt: What did your uncle say about his trip abroad?
questions.fullSentence: He said he was already considering going back.
questions.explanation: 'Planning' is extra. 'Considering going back' is the natural expression used here.

questions.prompt: What did the tour guide tell visitors about photography?
questions.fullSentence: She told us not to take photos inside the hall.
questions.explanation: 'Flash' is the extra word. The restriction is on all photography, not just flash.

questions.prompt: What did your friend ask when you mentioned the concert?
questions.fullSentence: He asked if there were still any tickets left.
questions.explanation: 'More' is the extra word. 'Any tickets left' already covers the question.

questions.prompt: What did the school newsletter announce?
questions.fullSentence: It announced that new courses would be offered next semester.
questions.explanation: 'Added' is the extra word. 'Offered' is the correct verb for courses.

questions.prompt: What did the nurse tell you before the procedure?
questions.fullSentence: She told me not to eat anything for two hours.
questions.explanation: 'Fast' is the extra word. 'Not to eat anything' directly states the instruction.

questions.prompt: What did your gym instructor say about the new schedule?
questions.fullSentence: He said classes would now be held on Saturdays.
questions.explanation: 'Offered' is the extra word. 'Held on Saturdays' is the correct phrasing for scheduled classes.

tips: Read the question first — it tells you what the sentence is trying to say.
Look at what's already fixed in the sentence — it helps narrow down the structure.
Watch for reporting verbs: 'She said that...', 'He asked if...', 'They told me to...'
The extra word is usually a synonym of a word already used in the sentence.
Say the completed sentence in your head — if it sounds natural, it's likely correct.
whatToExpect: A question about what someone said, asked, or announced.
A partial sentence with some words already fixed and blanks to fill.
A set of scrambled words — one of which is extra and should NOT be used.
Sentences focus on reported speech: told, asked, said, mentioned, warned, reminded.
35 questions total — take your time, there is no timer in practice mode.
